document.title: Software Validation Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: Content of Premarket Submissions for Device Software Functions (June 14, 2023)
Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices (May 11, 2005)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for GT1717C and GT1717CP Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector SDK and onboard firmware/FPGA software.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software validation, verification, hazard analysis, traceability, lifecycle documentation, and unresolved anomaly reporting for regulatory technical documentation.
keyPoints: The GT1717C and GT1717CP differ by internal CsI deposition thickness, but the software and firmware are the same.
The report concludes a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software.
The detector software consists of an SDK and onboard module firmware/FPGA functions for image acquisition, correction, preprocessing, communication, and firmware update.
Module verification and system validation test tables show pass results for common functions, image acquisition, calibration, firmware update, CPU firmware, FPGA app module, and error control.
Hazard analysis lists communication, x-ray exposure energy, image correction, firmware, shock, parameter, sensor, login, and data encryption hazards with risk controls.
Unresolved anomalies are listed as None.

entities.name: Quality Manager
entities.evidence: Approval Quality Manager Hur, Namkyu March 23, 2026

entities.name: SDK
entities.evidence: SDK (Software Development Kit): A collection of functions that acquire detector images and perform various manipulations.

entities.name: Onboard Module
entities.evidence: Onboard Module (Firmware/FPGA): This program, embedded in the detector, communicates with the PC console and acquires images from the detector's internal FPGA connected to the panel

concepts.name: Software level of concern
concepts.evidence: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software. Level of Concern: Moderate

concepts.name: Software architecture design
concepts.evidence: SDK/On Boards Module Software architecture consists of five independent modules.

concepts.name: Calibration
concepts.evidence: Calibration performs following compensations to acquired images.

concepts.name: Offset correction
concepts.evidence: Offset Correction Obtain dark image which noise is filtered. And then, subtract each pixel value of dark image from real X-ray acquired image.

concepts.name: Gain correction
concepts.evidence: This process reduces brightness variations across the entire image and achieves a uniform background brightness.

concepts.name: Defect correction
concepts.evidence: extract corresponding area and compensate through dust remover and defect correction algorithm.

evidence.claim: The document is a Software Validation Report for the GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The covered product models are GT1717C and GT1717CP.
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: GT1717C, GT1717CP

evidence.claim: The report number is A-SVR-CYN (Rev.1).
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-SVR-CYN (Rev.1)

evidence.claim: The prepared, reviewed, and approval dates shown on the signature page are March 23, 2026.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Song, Jinsun March 23, 2026 Reviewed R&D Team Manager Lee Kyung Soo March 23, 2026 Approval Quality Manager Hur, Namkyu March 23, 2026

evidence.claim: The GT1717C and GT1717CP have the same software and firmware.
evidence.evidenceText: The difference between the GT1717C and GT1717CP products is the internal CsI deposition thickness, but the software and firmware are the same.

evidence.claim: The report concludes a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software.
evidence.evidenceText: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software. Level of Concern: Moderate

evidence.claim: The level of concern section references FDA software premarket submission guidance.
evidence.evidenceText: The following section includes the information required in the FDA guidance document entitled “Content of Premarket Submissions for Device Software Functions” (June 14, 2023)

evidence.claim: The software development environment description states that the development process fulfills 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

evidence.claim: The detector software is divided into SDK and Onboard Module firmware/FPGA.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector software can be broadly divided into two categories. 1. SDK (Software Development Kit) ... 2. Onboard Module (Firmware/FPGA)

evidence.claim: The SDK includes common functions, image acquisition, calibration, and firmware update modules.
evidence.evidenceText: Common functions module: Control detector setup Image acquisition module: Control image read Calibration module: Calibration setup and correction or pre-processing control of image Firmware update: Write detector firmware update file and read information

evidence.claim: The onboard module includes CPU module and FPGA app module roles.
evidence.evidenceText: CPU Module: System initialization and task creation. And power device and shock sensor communication control and initialization FPGA App Module: Image reading and communication message processing in FPGA

evidence.claim: The software supports Windows 10 or higher, i5 8th generation or higher, and 8GB DDR4 RAM or higher as system requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: System requirement OS: Windows 10 or higher CPU: i5 8th generation or higher RAM: 8GB DDR4 or higher

evidence.claim: Detector hardware requirements include Intel Cyclone V, 2GB volatile memory, 8GB flash memory, network interfaces, and Embedded Linux 32-bit.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector H/W Device requirement CPU: Intel Cyclone V Volatile Memory: 2GB Flash Memory: 8GB NIC: 10/100/1000 network system, 802.11 a/b/g/n/ac. OS: Embedded Linux 32-bit

evidence.claim: The development language is Microsoft Visual Professional 2022, C#.
evidence.evidenceText: Program Language Microsoft Visual Professional 2022, C#

evidence.claim: The software lifecycle follows a classic waterfall methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process at software follows a classic waterfall methodology

evidence.claim: Module verification positively evaluated successful installation of the software on a standard PC.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of module verification, we positively evaluate the success in installed Software on standard PC.

evidence.claim: System testing found no unresolved bugs affecting safety and efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: System testing verifies that all functions configured in each module are adequate. No unresolved bugs have been found in this software that affect safety and efficiency.

evidence.claim: The unresolved anomalies list states None.
evidence.evidenceText: Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability is divided into acceptable and unacceptable regions, with risk levels 1 to 4 acceptable and 5 to 25 unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable – Light Gray Zone (Risk Level 1 ~ 4) In this zone, risk is acceptable. Unacceptable – Dark Gray Zone (Risk Level 5 ~ 25) Elimination or mitigation actions must be taken

evidence.claim: The hazard analysis includes cybersecurity hazards for login and data encryption.
evidence.evidenceText: HA-0009 Login Unauthorized access by unauthorized personnel Cybersecurity ... HA-0010 Data encryption Unauthorized user can access the unencrypted data Cybersecurity

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata and file name indicate Rev.2, but the document text states Report No.: A-SVR-CYN (Rev.1).
Authority is ambiguous: source metadata indicates MFDS/domestic, while the document text cites FDA guidance and 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
Revision record table includes April 14, 2026 entries, while the signature page date is March 23, 2026.
Some traceability entries appear inconsistent or abbreviated, including SV2-17, SV2-18, SV2-19, SV2-20 and FPGA rows using SDS-7000/SV1-7000 while other sections use SDS-6000/SV1-6000.
